visa_types.name: Tourism / Other Private Visits
visa_types.documents_required:
Travel insurance is mandatory for all Schengen countries. If you have not purchased, please click here (https://visa.vfsglobal.com/rus/en/bgr/travel-insurance).
To get detailed information about documents required for tourism and other private visits purpose of travel please click  here (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/4zOol94rKt6wXzrjN6eKki/a440c73489b82c9121a69444aa91b4ff/tourism-checklist-feb-2022.pdf). 
- Visitors of military and civil burial grounds
- Property owners in the Schengen area and their close relatives
- Tourists and other private visitors
Russian Law on Data Protection
According to the Federal law of the Russian Federation №152-ФЗ “On personal data” all applicants should sign “The agreement on processing personal data”. Please note that an applicant who submits his/her documents through an authorized representative should prepare this document in advance or provide a power of attorney giving representative a right to sign it on his/her behalf. 
Please choose the link in accordance with visa application center where you are going to submit your visa application: 
- Agreement on processing personal data (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/2suxSsGEaz07MNIQMFtMAh/cb6abe4a7a6104aac4d3d38087582bc3/consent-to-vfs-professional-processing.pdf)
Additional information for minors: 
- Personal data subject – please enter child’s details;
- Representative of personal data subject – please enter details of the parent submitting application;
- Acting on behalf of the personal data subject on the basis of – please enter birth certificate details.
The agreement on processing personal data should be completed in accordance with internal passport. The agreement completed based on foreign passport cannot be accepted.
Please click on one of the links below to download required forms:
Sponsorship  letter (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/6XwAf1FbmgUFLLQDfd72y8/e57d9d14ce737fd88dd47506d89d5764/sponsor-letter-new.pdf)
Revocation  form (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/4sIRIaR60CDhZEZDndP5iX/45250ea4eaff7460e9b9acc9f64ca57d/claim-form-document.pdf)
Applicants can request to revoke their valid visas while submission new application only if these valid visas do not fully cover the first planned trip in accordance with supporting documents provided. 
If valid visa expires before the planned trip revocation form should be necessarily provided since the Consulate does not accept passports with valid visas for processing.
visa_types.visa_fees:
Visa Fees for short term Schengen Visa (Visa C):
- Visa Fee for citizens of Russian Federation and for citizens of countries, which do not have Visa Facilitation Agreement with EU is 90 Euro.
- Visa Fee for citizens of countries, which have Visa Facilitation Agreement with EU is 35 Euro.
VFS service fee for short term Schengen Visa (Visa C):
- In addition to the visa fee, visa centers charge a service fee of 22.8 euros, including VAT, for each application (payment is made in rubles at the exchange rate of the Embassy of the Republic of Bulgaria in Russia).
Visa Fee is free for the following categories of  applicants:
- Children up  to 12 years
visa_types.processing_time:
Standard visa application processing time is 5 working days exclusive of submission date, means from the date of delivery of application to the Consulate of Bulgaria in Ekaterinburg (processing times of regional applications can vary as it depends of delivery schedule).
Urgent visa processing time is 3 working days exclusive of submission date.
Please note that as per the Agreement between the European Community and the Russian Federation processing time of a visa application can be up to 30 working days, especially if there is a necessity in its additional assessment.

visa_types.overview:
Overview
- Currently Bulgaria issues national visas which allow their holders to enter and stay not only in Bulgaria but also in Romania, Croatia and Cyprus. Bulgaria visas do not allow their holders to enter and stay in Schengen countries.
- Bulgaria Visa application center accepts only visa applications for short term visas (C visas). C visas can be one, double and multiple entry. Duration of stay on the territory of  Bulgaria cannot exceed 90 days within 180 day period irrespective of visa duration. All the above listed documents are obligatory for submission. Incomplete application package cannot be accepted.
Visa application can be submitted in person, by close relative or by representative:
- In Person (adult applicants only)
- It is necessary to provide internal passport.
- By close relative
Close relatives include spouses, parents, children (including adopted), grandparents, grandchildren. Sisters and brothers as well as other relatives can submit visa applications for each other only based on notarized power of attorney.
Your close relative should provide the following documents:
- Internal passport in original + copy of information page;
- Documents proving relationship: birth certificate, marriage certificate, divorce certificate, name change certificate issued by the Civil Registry Office. Marriage stamp in internal passport as well as information about children are not considered proof of relationship. All certificates should be provided in original or notarized copy. Moreover copies of these documents should be also provided.
- By representative:
- on the basis of notarized power of attorney
Your representative should provide the following documents:
- Internal passport in original + copy of information page;
- Notarized power of attorney for submission documents (original + copy); it should clearly states that authorized representative (full name) is eligible to submit visa application for Bulgaria visa in the Consulate of Bulgaria (or in Bulgaria visa application center).
Power of attorney should contain full name of applicant(s). There should not be any mistakes or corrections. Visa application cannot be accepted without original of power of attorney.
- on the basis of corporate power of attorney (only for business/service/sport/culture purpose of travel)
The power of attorney should be on a headed paper, with a stamp and signature of the head of the company which sends you on a trip. The power of attorney should also contain your passport details and passports details of your colleague who will submit visa application on your behalf.
Your representative should provide the following documents:
- Internal passport in original + copy of information page;
- Power of attorney for document submission (original + copy); visa application will not be accepted without original of power of attorney;
- Certificate of employment (if this is not stated in power of attorney).
- Citizens of those countries who need a visa to visit Bulgaria can also enter the country using valid Schengen visa, Cyprus, Romania or Croatia visa. Number of entries and period of stay will correspond with the ones stated in these visas but not more than 90 days in 180 day period. If applicant still wants to apply documents for Bulgaria visa, visa application centers will accept them.
- To learn more about entering Bulgaria using Schengen visa please click here (http://www.mfa.bg/embassies/russia/ll/987/index.html)
.
- You can also enter Bulgaria using valid visa issued in his/her revoked passport on condition that new international passport is also presented. You can enter Bulgaria using Bulgaria, Schengen, Romania, Croatia or Cyprus (except for pro-visas) visa. If applicant wants a visa to be issued in his/her new valid passport visa application center can accept application together with a revocation form and a revoked passport.
- Foreign citizens can apply for Bulgarian visa in case they legally stay on the territory of the Russian Federation on temporary or permanent basis (residence permit or registration for the whole period of visa validity should be provided). Moreover foreign applicants should prove that they legally stay on the territory of the RF with additional documents.  Depending on the situation it can be marriage certificate, company registration documents, work permit, patent, etc. It is also required to submit copies of previous Bulgaria and Schengen visas for the last 3 years. Standard processing time is up to 10 days but please note that as per the Agreement between the European Community and the Russian Federation processing time of a visa application can be up to 30 working days, especially if there is a necessity in its additional assessment.
- Citizens of Moldova, Georgia, Montenegro, Macedonia, Albania, Serbia, Bosnia and Herzegovina, and Ukraine who have biometric foreign passports can enter and stay in Bulgaria without  a visa for 90 days within 180 day period. Citizens of the above mentioned countries who do not have biometric passports should apply for a Bulgarian visa as per the standard procedure.
- To learn more about rules of calculation of authorized stay in Bulgaria click here (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/5ZYQ57ZGhqu80xldJuOchm/0b061c2c7605a36723ab1c9fcf958de7/rule-counting-new.pdf)
.

## Visa Fees
Visa Fees for short term Schengen Visa (Visa C):
- Visa Fee for citizens of Russian Federation and for citizens of countries, which do not have Visa Facilitation Agreement with EU is 90 Euro.
- Visa Fee for citizens of countries, which have Visa Facilitation Agreement with EU is 35 Euro.
VFS service fee for short term Schengen Visa (Visa C):
- In addition to the visa fee, visa centers charge a service fee of 22.8 euros, including VAT, for each application (payment is made in rubles at the exchange rate of the Embassy of the Republic of Bulgaria in Russia).
Visa Fee is free for the following categories of  applicants:
- Children up  to 12 years

## Documents Required
Travel insurance is mandatory for all Schengen countries. If you have not purchased, please click here (https://visa.vfsglobal.com/rus/en/bgr/travel-insurance).
To get detailed information about documents required for tourism and other private visits purpose of travel please click  here (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/4zOol94rKt6wXzrjN6eKki/a440c73489b82c9121a69444aa91b4ff/tourism-checklist-feb-2022.pdf). 
- Visitors of military and civil burial grounds
- Property owners in the Schengen area and their close relatives
- Tourists and other private visitors

- Acting on behalf of the personal data subject on the basis of – please enter birth certificate details.
The agreement on processing personal data should be completed in accordance with internal passport. The agreement completed based on foreign passport cannot be accepted.
Please click on one of the links below to download required forms:
Sponsorship  letter (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/6XwAf1FbmgUFLLQDfd72y8/e57d9d14ce737fd88dd47506d89d5764/sponsor-letter-new.pdf)
Revocation  form (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/4sIRIaR60CDhZEZDndP5iX/45250ea4eaff7460e9b9acc9f64ca57d/claim-form-document.pdf)

## Photo Specifications
General  information 
- Photo should be colored, full-faced and without fringing.
- Photo should have been taken within the last six months.
- Each applicant should submit 1 photo along with visa application.
Photo  size 
- 35 x 45 mm with face centered in the frame
- Head (from the top of the head to the bottom of the chin) should be about 30 mm.
Photo  specifications 
- Photo should be taken fullface with applicant facing camera directly.
- Face should cover about 70-80% of the photo.
- Photo should be colored and have white or light background. Photos with dark or patterned background will not be accepted.
- Face should be clearly seen. It is preferable to have ears open.
- Sunglasses or other accessories are not acceptable unless required for medical reasons (e.g. eye patch).
- Photo depicting a person wearing a traditional facemask or a veil that does not permit adequate identification is not acceptable.
Please  note: 
Kindly follow the instructions above. If photos provided do not correspond with the Consulate requirements your application pack will be considered incomplete. In case you do not have
                            an opportunity to make a photo in advance you can do it in visa application center.

## Processing Time
Standard visa application processing time is 5 working days exclusive of submission date, means from the date of delivery of application to the Consulate of Bulgaria in Ekaterinburg (processing times of regional applications can vary as it depends of delivery schedule).
Urgent visa processing time is 3 working days exclusive of submission date.
Please note that as per the Agreement between the European Community and the Russian Federation processing time of a visa application can be up to 30 working days, especially if there is a necessity in its additional assessment.

                              Schengen, Romania, Croatia or Cyprus (except for pro-visas) visa. If applicant wants a visa to be issued in his/her new valid passport visa application center can accept application
                              together with a revocation form and a revoked passport.
- Foreign citizens can apply for Bulgarian visa in case they legally stay on the territory of the Russian Federation on temporary or permanent basis (residence permit or registration
                              for the whole period of visa validity should be provided). Moreover foreign applicants should prove that they legally stay on the territory of the RF with additional documents.
                              Depending on the situation it can be marriage certificate, company registration documents, work permit, patent, etc. It is also required to submit copies of previous Bulgaria
                              and Schengen visas for the last 3 years. Standard processing time is up to 10 days but please note that as per the Agreement between the European Community and the Russian Federation
                              processing time of a visa application can be up to 30 working days, especially if there is a necessity in its additional assessment.
- Citizens of Moldova, Georgia, Montenegro, Macedonia, Albania, Serbia, Bosnia and Herzegovina, and Ukraine who have biometric foreign passports can enter and stay in Bulgaria without
                              a visa for 90 days within 180 day period. Citizens of the above mentioned countries who do not have biometric passports should apply for a Bulgarian visa as per the standard procedure.
- To learn more about rules of calculation of authorized stay in Bulgaria click here (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/5ZYQ57ZGhqu80xldJuOchm/0b061c2c7605a36723ab1c9fcf958de7/rule-counting-new.pdf).

## Visa Fees
Visa Fees for short term Schengen Visa (Visa C):
- Visa Fee for citizens of Russian Federation and for citizens of countries, which do not have Visa Facilitation Agreement with EU is 90 Euro.
- Visa Fee for citizens of countries, which have Visa Facilitation Agreement with EU is 35 Euro.
VFS service fee for short term Schengen Visa (Visa C):
- In addition to the visa fee, visa centers charge a service fee of 22.8 euros, including VAT, for each application (payment is made in rubles at the exchange rate of the Embassy of the Republic of Bulgaria in Russia).
Visa Fee is free for the following categories of  applicants:
- Children up  to 12 years

## Photo Specifications
General  information 
- Photo should be colored, full-faced and without fringing.
- Photo should have been taken within the last six months.
- Each applicant should submit 1 photo along with visa application.
Photo  size 
- 35 x 45 mm with face centered in the frame
- Head (from the top of the head to the bottom of the chin) should be about 30 mm.
Photo  specifications 
- Photo should be taken fullface with applicant facing camera directly.
- Face should cover about 70-80% of the photo.
- Photo should be colored and have white or light background. Photos with dark or patterned background will not be accepted.
- Face should be clearly seen. It is preferable to have ears open.
- Sunglasses or other accessories are not acceptable unless required for medical reasons (e.g. eye patch).
- Photo depicting a person wearing a traditional facemask or a veil that does not permit adequate identification is not acceptable.
Please  note: 
Kindly follow the instructions above. If photos provided do not correspond with the Consulate requirements your application pack will be considered incomplete. In case you do not have
                            an opportunity to make a photo in advance you can do it in visa application center.

## Processing Time
Standard visa application processing time is 5 working days exclusive of submission date, means from the date of delivery of application to the Consulate of Bulgaria in Ekaterinburg (processing times of regional applications can vary as it depends of delivery schedule).
Urgent visa processing time is 3 working days exclusive of submission date.
Please note that as per the Agreement between the European Community and the Russian Federation processing time of a visa application can be up to 30 working days, especially if there is a necessity in its additional assessment.

Incomplete application package    cannot be accepted.
Visa application can be submitted in person, by close relative or by representative:
- In Person (adult applicants only)
- It is necessary to provide internal passport.
- By close relative
Close relatives include spouses, parents, children (including adopted), grandparents, grandchildren. Sisters and brothers as well as other relatives can submit visa applications for each other only based on notarized power of attorney.
Your close relative should provide the following documents:
- Internal passport in original + copy of information page;
- Documents proving relationship: birth certificate, marriage certificate, divorce certificate, name change certificate issued by the Civil Registry Office. Marriage stamp in internal passport as well as information about children are not considered proof of relationship. All certificates should be provided in original or notarized copy. Moreover copies of these documents should be also provided.
- By representative:
- on the basis of notarized power of attorney
Your representative should provide the following documents:
- Internal passport in original + copy of information page;
- Notarized power of attorney for submission documents (original + copy); it should clearly states that authorized representative (full name) is eligible to submit visa application for Bulgaria visa in the Consulate of Bulgaria (or in Bulgaria visa application center).
Power of attorney should contain full name of applicant(s). There should not be any mistakes or corrections. Visa application cannot be accepted without original of power of attorney.
- on the basis of corporate power of attorney (only for business/service/sport/culture purpose of travel)
The power of attorney should be on a headed paper, with a stamp and signature of the head of the company which sends you on a trip. The power of attorney should also contain your passport details and passports details of your colleague who will submit visa application on your behalf.
Your representative should provide the following documents:
- Internal passport in original + copy of information page;
- Power of attorney for document submission (original + copy); visa application will not be accepted without original of power of attorney;

visa_types.documents_required:
Travel insurance is mandatory for all Schengen countries. If you have not purchased, please click here (https://visa.vfsglobal.com/rus/en/bgr/travel-insurance).
To get detailed information about documents required please chose your purpose of travel:
- Close relatives visiting Russian citizens residing legally in a Schengen Member State (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/3sAfEdE6CNc9XeU7P9i0Ek/c146a114214d1a7fc4fbce08892fbafb/close-relatives-checklist-feb-2022.pdf)
- Close relatives visiting EU (EEA & Swiss) citizens (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/3sAfEdE6CNc9XeU7P9i0Ek/c146a114214d1a7fc4fbce08892fbafb/close-relatives-checklist-feb-2022.pdf)
- Family members of EU (EEA & Swiss) citizens (not resident in their home country in line with [Directive 2004/38/EC) (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/3sAfEdE6CNc9XeU7P9i0Ek/c146a114214d1a7fc4fbce08892fbafb/close-relatives-checklist-feb-2022.pdf)
Russian Law on Data Protection
According to the Federal law of the Russian Federation №152-ФЗ “On personal data” all applicants should sign “The agreement on processing personal data”. Please note that an applicant who submits his/her documents through an authorized representative should prepare this document in advance or provide a power of attorney giving representative a right to sign it on his/her behalf.
Please choose the link in accordance with visa application center where you are going to submit your visa application:
- Agreement on processing personal data (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/2suxSsGEaz07MNIQMFtMAh/cb6abe4a7a6104aac4d3d38087582bc3/consent-to-vfs-professional-processing.pdf)
Additional information for minors:
- Personal data subject – please enter child’s details;
- Representative of personal data subject – please enter details of the parent submitting application;
- Acting on behalf of the personal data subject on the basis of – please enter birth certificate details.
The agreement on processing personal data should be completed in accordance with internal passport. The agreement completed based on foreign passport cannot be accepted.
Please click on one of the links below to download required forms:
Sponsorship  letter (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/6XwAf1FbmgUFLLQDfd72y8/e57d9d14ce737fd88dd47506d89d5764/sponsor-letter-new.pdf)

Applicants can request to revoke their valid visas while submission new application only if these valid visas do not fully cover the first planned trip in accordance with supporting documents  provided. 
If valid visa expires before the planned trip revocation form should be necessarily provided since the Consulate does not accept passports with valid visas for processing.

- Power of attorney for document submission (original + copy); visa application will not be accepted without original of power of attorney;
- Certificate of employment (if this is not stated in power of attorney).
- Citizens of those countries who need a visa to visit Bulgaria can also enter the country using valid Schengen visa, Cyprus, Romania or Croatia visa. Number of entries and period of stay will correspond with the ones stated in these visas but not more than 90 days in 180 day period. If applicant still wants to apply documents for Bulgaria visa, visa application centers will accept them.
- To learn more about entering Bulgaria using Schengen visa please click here (http://www.mfa.bg/embassies/russia/ll/987/index.html)
.
- You can also enter Bulgaria using valid visa issued in his/her revoked passport on condition that new international passport is also presented. You can enter Bulgaria using Bulgaria, Schengen, Romania, Croatia or Cyprus (except for pro-visas) visa. If applicant wants a visa to be issued in his/her new valid passport visa application center can accept application  together with a revocation form and a revoked passport.
- Foreign citizens can apply for Bulgarian visa in case they legally stay on the territory of the Russian Federation on temporary or permanent basis (residence permit or registration  for the whole period of visa validity should be provided). Moreover foreign applicants should prove that they legally stay on the territory of the RF with additional documents.  Depending on the situation it can be marriage certificate, company registration documents, work permit, patent, etc. It is also required to submit copies of previous Bulgaria  and Schengen visas for the last 3 years. Standard processing time is up to 10 days but please note that as per the Agreement between the European Community and the Russian Federation processing time of a visa application can be up to 30 working days, especially if there is a necessity in its additional assessment.
- Citizens of Moldova, Georgia, Montenegro, Macedonia, Albania, Serbia, Bosnia and Herzegovina, and Ukraine who have biometric foreign passports can enter and stay in Bulgaria without a visa for 90 days within 180 day period. Citizens of the above mentioned countries who do not have biometric passports should apply for a Bulgarian visa as per the standard procedure.
- To learn more about rules of calculation of authorized stay in Bulgaria click here (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/5ZYQ57ZGhqu80xldJuOchm/0b061c2c7605a36723ab1c9fcf958de7/rule-counting-new.pdf)
.

.

## Visa Fees
Visa Fees for short term Schengen Visa (Visa C):
- Visa Fee for citizens of Russian Federation and for citizens of countries, which do not have Visa Facilitation Agreement with EU is 90 Euro.
- Visa Fee for citizens of countries, which have Visa Facilitation Agreement with EU is 35 Euro.
VFS service fee for short term Schengen Visa (Visa C):
- In addition to the visa fee, visa centers charge a service fee of 22.8 euros, including VAT, for each application (payment is made in rubles at the exchange rate of the Embassy of the Republic of Bulgaria in Russia).
Visa Fee is free for the following categories of  applicants:
- Children up  to 12 years

## Documents Required
Travel insurance is mandatory for all Schengen countries. If you have not purchased, please click here (https://visa.vfsglobal.com/rus/en/bgr/travel-insurance).
To get detailed information about documents required please chose your purpose of travel:
- Close relatives visiting Russian citizens residing legally in a Schengen Member State (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/3sAfEdE6CNc9XeU7P9i0Ek/c146a114214d1a7fc4fbce08892fbafb/close-relatives-checklist-feb-2022.pdf)
- Close relatives visiting EU (EEA & Swiss) citizens (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/3sAfEdE6CNc9XeU7P9i0Ek/c146a114214d1a7fc4fbce08892fbafb/close-relatives-checklist-feb-2022.pdf)
- Family members of EU (EEA & Swiss) citizens (not resident in their home country in line with [Directive 2004/38/EC) (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/3sAfEdE6CNc9XeU7P9i0Ek/c146a114214d1a7fc4fbce08892fbafb/close-relatives-checklist-feb-2022.pdf)
Russian Law on Data Protection
According to the Federal law of the Russian Federation №152-ФЗ “On personal data” all applicants should sign “The agreement on processing personal data”. Please note that an applicant who submits his/her documents through an authorized representative should prepare this document in advance or provide a power of attorney giving representative a right to sign it on his/her behalf.
Please choose the link in accordance with visa application center where you are going to submit your visa application:
- Agreement on processing personal data (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/2suxSsGEaz07MNIQMFtMAh/cb6abe4a7a6104aac4d3d38087582bc3/consent-to-vfs-professional-processing.pdf)
Additional information for minors:
- Personal data subject – please enter child’s details;
- Representative of personal data subject – please enter details of the parent submitting application;
- Acting on behalf of the personal data subject on the basis of – please enter birth certificate details.
The agreement on processing personal data should be completed in accordance with internal passport. The agreement completed based on foreign passport cannot be accepted.
Please click on one of the links below to download required forms:
Sponsorship  letter (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/6XwAf1FbmgUFLLQDfd72y8/e57d9d14ce737fd88dd47506d89d5764/sponsor-letter-new.pdf)
Revocation  form (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/4sIRIaR60CDhZEZDndP5iX/45250ea4eaff7460e9b9acc9f64ca57d/claim-form-document.pdf)
Applicants can request to revoke their valid visas while submission new application only if these valid visas do not fully cover the first planned trip in accordance with supporting documents  provided. 
If valid visa expires before the planned trip revocation form should be necessarily provided since the Consulate does not accept passports with valid visas for processing.

## Photo Specifications
General  information 
- Photo should be colored, full-faced and without fringing.
- Photo should have been taken within the last six months.
- Each applicant should submit 1 photo along with visa application.
Photo  size 
- 35 x 45 mm with face centered in the frame
- Head (from the top of the head to the bottom of the chin) should be about 30 mm.
Photo  specifications 
- Photo should be taken fullface with applicant facing camera directly.
- Face should cover about 70-80% of the photo.
- Photo should be colored and have white or light background. Photos with dark or patterned background will not be accepted.
- Face should be clearly seen. It is preferable to have ears open.
- Sunglasses or other accessories are not acceptable unless required for medical reasons (e.g. eye patch).
- Photo depicting a person wearing a traditional facemask or a veil that does not permit adequate identification is not acceptable.
Please  note: 
Kindly follow the instructions above. If photos provided do not correspond with the Consulate requirements your application pack will be considered incomplete. In case you do not have
                            an opportunity to make a photo in advance you can do it in visa application center.

## Processing Time
Standard visa application processing time is 5 working days exclusive of submission date, means from the date of delivery of application to the Consulate of Bulgaria  in Ekaterinburg (processing times of regional applications can vary as it depends of delivery schedule).
Urgent visa processing time is 3 working days exclusive of submission date.
Please note that as per the Agreement between the European Community and the Russian Federation processing time of a visa application can be up to 30 working days, especially if there is a necessity in its additional assessment.

## Download Forms
Dear applicants, please note that all Bulgaria visa application centers offer assisted form filling service. 
Please refer to our page “Additional Services (http://www.vfsglobal.com/bulgaria/russia/Ekaterinburg/English/additional-services.html#5)”  to check the fee applicable for this service. 
To download a document required please choose one of the links below:
Visa  application form (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/3gnWPHSxeLp4WMcP0lpovO/cf4500c9532e96bb56a4d0796f13b35a/appl-viza-rs.doc)
Instructions  for completing visa application form (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/7jZccB8KKCJVTkoe2ZRjFF/d653277318f114fa9461f4b3346558ab/form-filling-instruction-bulgaria.pdf)
Sample visa  application form (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/5FhJMcpk964GzZb6xF3zLG/b06bfbadeb7671794d597ce23041192f/d-form-for-invited-persons.pdf)
To download a visa application form:
- Click the link;
- When application form opens, save it on your computer.
OR
- Right click on the required link;
- Choose the option “Save as”;
- Save application form on your computer.
Please  note:
- Visa application form should be on 3 pages.
- It should be printed both sides.

- Representative of personal data subject – please enter details of the parent submitting application;

The agreement on processing personal data should be completed in accordance with internal passport. The agreement completed based on foreign passport cannot be accepted.
Please click on one of the links below to download required forms: 
Sponsorship  letter (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/6XwAf1FbmgUFLLQDfd72y8/e57d9d14ce737fd88dd47506d89d5764/sponsor-letter-new.pdf)
Revocation  form (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/4sIRIaR60CDhZEZDndP5iX/45250ea4eaff7460e9b9acc9f64ca57d/claim-form-document.pdf)
Applicants can request to revoke their valid visas while submission new application only if these valid visas do not fully cover the first planned trip in accordance with supporting documents provided. 
If valid visa expires before the planned trip revocation form should be necessarily provided since the Consulate does not accept passports with valid visas for processing.

## Photo Specifications
General  information 
- Photo should be colored, full-faced and without fringing.
- Photo should have been taken within the last six months.
- Each applicant should submit 1 photo along with visa application.
Photo  size 
- 35 x 45 mm with face centered in the frame
- Head (from the top of the head to the bottom of the chin) should be about 30 mm.
Photo  specifications 
- Photo should be taken fullface with applicant facing camera directly.
- Face should cover about 70-80% of the photo.
- Photo should be colored and have white or light background. Photos with dark or patterned background will not be accepted.
- Face should be clearly seen. It is preferable to have ears open.
- Sunglasses or other accessories are not acceptable unless required for medical reasons (e.g. eye patch).
- Photo depicting a person wearing a traditional facemask or a veil that does not permit adequate identification is not acceptable.
Please  note: 
Kindly follow the instructions above. If photos provided do not correspond with the Consulate requirements your application pack will be considered incomplete. In case you do not have
                            an opportunity to make a photo in advance you can do it in visa application center.

## Processing Time
Standard visa application processing time is 5 working days exclusive of submission date, means from the date of delivery of application to the Consulate of Bulgaria
                            in Ekaterinburg (processing times of regional applications can vary as it depends of delivery schedule).
Urgent visa processing time is 3 working days exclusive of submission date.
Please note that as per the Agreement between the European Community and the Russian Federation processing time of a visa application can be up to 30 working days, especially if there
                            is a necessity in its additional assessment.

## Download Forms
Dear applicants, please note that all Bulgaria visa application centers offer assisted form filling service. 
Please refer to our page “Additional Services (http://www.vfsglobal.com/bulgaria/russia/Ekaterinburg/English/additional-services.html#5)” to check the fee applicable for this service. 
To download a document required please choose one of the links below:
Visa  application form (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/3gnWPHSxeLp4WMcP0lpovO/cf4500c9532e96bb56a4d0796f13b35a/appl-viza-rs.doc)
Instructions  for completing visa application form (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/7jZccB8KKCJVTkoe2ZRjFF/d653277318f114fa9461f4b3346558ab/form-filling-instruction-bulgaria.pdf)
To download a visa application form:
- Click the link;
- When application form opens, save it on your computer.
OR
- Right click on the required link;
- Choose the option “Save as”;
- Save application form on your computer.
Please  note:
- Visa application form should be on 3 pages.
- It should be printed both sides.
